methodsAttempted.query: site:uoregon.edu (AI OR "artificial intelligence" OR ChatGPT OR "generative AI") policy students teaching privacy "University of Oregon"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the central Teaching Support AI guide, AI academic integrity page, UO Libraries research guide, news/event pages, and assessment pages.
Firecrawl search found the official Policy Library Student Conduct Code and a Teaching Support academic-integrity explainer.
Firecrawl search found the UO Service Portal AI Service Offerings and Microsoft Copilot Chat knowledge-base pages.
The central AI guide links to the AI FAQ, data privacy, academic integrity, and assessment resources.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found university-level AI guidance in Teaching Support and Innovation, the official Student Conduct Code in the Policy Library, and AI tool/security guidance in the UO Service Portal. News/event material was rejected as advisory or reporting rather than policy evidence; a UO Libraries research guide was not used for canonical claims because the Teaching Support and Service Portal pages provided stronger central policy and data-handling evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: AI Policy & Principled Practice for Instruction: FAQ

stopConditions: Stop after UO-controlled sources support narrow claim candidates.
Do not use news, events, research showcases, or course-specific pages as binding policy evidence.
Do not infer university-wide requirements from library guidance where stronger central Teaching Support or Service Portal sources exist.

artifacts.claimText: University of Oregon teaching guidance says instructors should have a syllabus policy statement explaining what generative AI use is allowed and disallowed in their course.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow teaching-guidance claim; the source says instructors should have a syllabus policy statement, not that a central university template is mandatory.
Approved as formal Student Conduct Code evidence. The claim keeps the source's conditional phrasing that AI-generated material may be included in plagiarism.
Approved as an academic-integrity guidance claim. The source cautions against reliance on detector percentages rather than banning every possible detector use.
Approved as privacy guidance from UO Information Services as republished on Teaching Support; the claim does not assert FERPA compliance.
Approved as an AI tool treatment claim from the Service Portal; tool status and data-risk limitation are scoped to the retrieved page.

artifacts.title: University of Oregon AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Draft evidence bundle for University of Oregon found central Teaching Support guidance, an official Student Conduct Code provision that includes AI-generated material in plagiarism examples, academic-integrity guidance cautioning against AI detector reliance, and Service Portal guidance on approved AI tools and low-risk data handling.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not marked canonical or human reviewed.
Course-specific, news, event, and lower-specificity advisory pages were not used as binding policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
